text_blocks.text: fragile, because the many delicate valleys separated by high
mountains often form unique ecologies. The mountainous
regions themselves are also vulnerable, with a rich range of
plants not found at other altitudes, and experience some
pressure from visitors and grazing. The climatic, geological
and topographical conditions of the alpine region make for
a very fragile ecosystem that is particularly sensitive to
climate change. Nevertheless, according to the 2014
Environmental Performance Index, Switzerland ranks first
among 132 nations in safeguarding the environment due to
its high scores on environmental public health, its heavy
reliance on renewable sources of energy (hydropower and
geothermal energy), and its control of greenhouse gas
